methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
The ePlane Company is a Series B transportation-sector venture that has raised $14M+ and is seeking $40–50M in this round. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of eight private companies in the same sector, including Series A and Series C stage equivalents (Einride at $1.8B, Harbinger at $500M, and REGENT Craft at $270M), with no public comparables available.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $14M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $27M. Peer-median analysis across the three closest comparable companies—filtered by transportation sector and same/adjacent stage—produces a $500M implied valuation, with a range spanning $270M to $1.8B. The disparity reflects the wide stage distribution and valuation variance within the transportation frontier-tech peer set.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $23M (low) to $575M (high), with a median of $263M. The wide spread reflects two distinct signals: stage-progression anchors conservatively to current capital deployment, while peer-median is pulled upward by later-stage comparable valuations. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage adjustment for a Series B in an unproven subsector and whether the peer set's later-stage companies genuinely reflect comparable risk profiles. The median estimate of $263M assumes the target will command valuation uplift relative to current capital raised but remains bounded by peer precedent.
